author: lia
content: Hey @marcus @dana — I know we're officially 'doing' both the stability patches and the upsell feature. Realistically, we're hitting the same subsystem and I don't want us tripping over each other. I'm drafting a trade-off doc for the Director to get us a clear 1-2 ranking so we aren't guessing which one matters more today.

author: marcus
content: @lia Glad you're drafting that. To be blunt: if we try to ship the upsell while the integration is this flaky, we're just building a faster way to trigger incidents. I'm focusing 100% on D1 until the 'bleeding' actually stops. Let me know when the Director picks a favorite child.

author: dana
content: @lia @marcus Marcus is right. The upsell code relies on the same hooks that are currently failing in prod. If I push the new feature now, I'm essentially pouring gasoline on the fire. I'm pausing active dev on D2 to help Marcus audit the integration paths so we have a stable foundation to build on.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Copy that. If the foundation is on fire, the upsell is dead on arrival anyway. I'm using your technical assessment to force a 'Sequence over Parallel' decision with the Director. Stick to D1 for now; I'll take the heat on the roadmap delay.

author: lia
content: The team is self-organizing around the fire, which is good, but I'm nervous about the Director's reaction to 'pausing' the revenue feature. I need to frame this as protecting the upsell's long-term success.

author: marcus
content: @lia Appreciate you backing us up. @dana, let's look at the trace logs from yesterday's spike—if we can harden the hook layer today, we'll have a much stronger case for the Director that we're actually making progress toward a platform that can support the upsell.

author: dana
content: @marcus Pulling the logs now. I'll map out where the upsell hooks intersect with the current failures so we can fix both at once. @lia Thanks for the cover—let us know if the Director needs a specific 'risk of failure' percentage to sign off on the sequence.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana I've sent the 'Sequence vs. Parallel' proposal to the Director. I used your point about the shared hooks—shipping the upsell on a broken base is a 100% failure risk. I'm asking for a formal sign-off on the 'Stability First' rank by EOD.

author: lia
content: I'm feeling the pressure, but the team's alignment makes the case stronger. I'm framing the delay as 'revenue protection'—if we ship a broken upsell, we lose the customer forever. High stakes, but it's the only honest path.

author: marcus
content: @lia Thanks. If they push back, tell them I'm already seeing 40% of the upsell's logic paths hitting the same deadlocks we're patching in D1. It's not a delay; it's a prerequisite.

author: dana
content: @lia Just to add some weight to your doc: I've confirmed that the upsell's state-machine depends on the exact same database locks that are currently timing out. If we don't fix D1 first, the upsell will literally be unusable for 30% of our high-volume users.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Director is reviewing the 'Sequence vs. Parallel' doc now. I've scheduled a sync for 4 PM to get the formal sign-off. I used your 'prerequisite' framing—it's the strongest lever we have. Stay focused on D1.

author: lia
content: Sending the calendar invite for the priority reset. I'm making it clear that 'Both' is a choice to fail at both. Stress is high, but the technical data from the team is solid.

author: marcus
content: @lia sounds good. I'm heads down on the deadlock patches for D1. If the Director needs a live demo of why the upsell would fail on the current build, I can have a test case ready by 4 PM to prove the point.

author: dana
content: Good call @marcus. I’ve got the trace data ready to show that the upsell’s 'must-have' features would trigger the exact same deadlock we're seeing in D1. @lia, if the Director asks about the timeline, tell them fixing the foundation now saves us two weeks of emergency hotfixing later.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Heading into the room now. I have your data on the database locks and the 'prerequisite' framing ready. I'll post the outcome here the second I have a signature on the re-ranked list.

author: lia
content: I'm refusing to leave the meeting without a ranked list. If they say 'both are P0,' I'm prepared to show the 100% failure risk data from Marcus. This is the moment for O1.

author: marcus
content: @lia Good luck. We're here if you need us to jump on the call to explain the lock contention. @dana, I'm pushing the first batch of guardrail tests now—can you double-check the retry logic on your end?

author: dana
content: @marcus On it. Reviewing the retry logic now. I'll make sure it handles the backoff gracefully so we don't accidentally DDOS our own hook layer once the locks clear.
